title: Snout and About: World Tapir Day
story: Every April 27, animal lovers around the world celebrate the tapir, one of nature's most unusual creatures! These pig-like animals with elephant trunks live in rainforests and love to swim. On World Tapir Day, zoos host special tapir talks and feeding demonstrations. Wildlife photographers share amazing tapir photos on social media, showing off their black and white markings. Conservation groups use this day to teach people about protecting tapir habitats. Many people discover for the first time that tapirs are actually related to horses and rhinos, not pigs!
activity_question: What animal are tapirs actually related to?
bonus_fact: Baby tapirs are born with spots and stripes that fade as they grow up!

title: Prime Rib Gets Its Day!
story: Meat lovers everywhere celebrate National Prime Rib Day on April 27! This juicy, tender cut of beef gets the spotlight as restaurants offer special deals and families fire up their grills. Prime rib is slow-roasted to perfection and often served with horseradish sauce or au jus for dipping. Food fans share photos of their perfectly cooked roasts online, making everyone hungry!
activity_question: What sauce is commonly served with prime rib?
bonus_fact: Prime rib comes from the same part of the cow as ribeye steaks!

title: National Tell a Story Day: Tales Come Alive!
story: Every April 27, storytellers everywhere celebrate National Tell a Story Day by sharing amazing tales with friends, family, and strangers! Libraries host special story hours where librarians use funny voices and props to bring characters to life. Teachers encourage students to write and share their own creative stories in class. Families gather around dinner tables to tell stories about their day or make up silly adventures together. Many people post short stories on social media or record themselves telling tales to share online. Some communities organize storytelling festivals where people of all ages take turns sharing everything from ghost stories to funny family memories. It's a day when imagination runs wild and everyone becomes a storyteller!
activity_question: Where do librarians often host special story hours on National Tell a Story Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most exciting story you've ever heard someone tell?

title: Swinging for the Fences on Babe Ruth Day
story: Baseball fans across America grab their gloves and bats to celebrate National Babe Ruth Day every April 27! This special day honors George Herman "Babe" Ruth, the legendary slugger who became baseball's biggest superstar. People visit baseball museums, watch classic game footage, and share their favorite Babe Ruth stories online. Many kids play pickup games in their backyards, trying to hit home runs just like the Babe did. Baseball teams often hold special ceremonies, and fans wear vintage jerseys to honor the Sultan of Swat. It's the perfect day to learn about baseball history while having fun with America's favorite pastime!
activity_question: What do baseball fans often wear on National Babe Ruth Day to honor the legendary player?
bonus_fact: Babe Ruth hit 714 home runs during his amazing career!

title: Freedom Day: South Africa's Big Celebration
story: Every April 27, South Africa celebrates Freedom Day with parades, music, and dancing in the streets! This special holiday marks when all South African people gained the right to vote, no matter their skin color. Families gather for braais (barbecues), schools hold special assemblies, and everyone wears the bright colors of their flag. It's a day to remember how far the country has come and celebrate unity together.
activity_question: What do South African families often do together on Freedom Day?
follow_up_question: What would you include in a Freedom Day celebration if you lived in South Africa?

title: Pampered Pups Get the Royal Treatment
story: Dogs across America get treated like furry royalty on April 27 during National Little Pampered Dog Day! Pet owners spoil their four-legged friends with extra belly rubs, gourmet treats, and maybe even a trip to the doggy spa. Some pups get new toys, cozy sweaters, or special photo shoots for social media. Dog bakeries see tons of customers buying "pupcakes" and bacon-flavored cookies. It's the perfect day to show your dog just how much they mean to you, whether they're tiny Chihuahuas or think they're lap dogs despite being Great Danes!
activity_question: What special treats might dogs get on National Little Pampered Dog Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most creative way you could pamper a dog for a day?

title: Dot-Dash-Celebrate: National Morse Code Day
story: Every April 27, people celebrate National Morse Code Day by tapping out secret messages using dots and dashes! This special communication system lets you spell words with just two simple signals - short beeps (dots) and long beeps (dashes). Kids love learning to spell their names in Morse code, while ham radio operators around the world send special messages to each other. Some people even blink flashlights in Morse code patterns to spell out words in the dark!
activity_question: What two signals are used to create Morse code messages?
bonus_fact: SOS in Morse code is three dots, three dashes, three dots: ...---...
